front: What is the primary goal of obtaining initial parameter estimates in time series model identification?
back: To provide starting values for nonlinear optimization algorithms, which then iteratively refine these estimates to find the values that minimize the sum of squared residuals.

front: How are initial estimates for the autoregressive (AR) parameters in an AR(p) model often obtained?
back: By using the Yule-Walker equations, which solve a system of equations linking the theoretical autocorrelations to the estimated sample autocorrelations (ρ̂_k) to get the AR coefficients.

front: How are initial estimates for the moving average (MA) parameters in an MA(q) model commonly derived?
back: By inverting the autocorrelation function (ACF). The theoretical ACF of an MA(q) process cuts off after lag q, and initial parameter values can be found by solving the system of equations relating the ACF to the parameters.

front: For a general ARMA(p,q) model, what method is frequently used to get initial parameter estimates?
back: A two-step process: 1) Fit a high-order AR model (e.g., via Yule-Walker) to approximate the ARMA structure and obtain initial AR estimates. 2) Use the residuals from this AR model to get initial estimates for the MA component.

front: Why are good initial estimates particularly important for MA and ARMA models compared to pure AR models?
back: Because the likelihood function for MA and ARMA models can be multi-modal (have multiple local minima). Poor initial estimates can cause the optimization algorithm to converge to a local optimum rather than the global optimum, leading to inferior model fits.
